title: Add tests for GroupQueryAttention (GQA) operations
description: Add tests verifying that GroupQueryAttention (com.microsoft.GroupQueryAttention) works through the EP. GQA is a variant of attention where num_heads for Q differs from kv_num_heads for K/V (each KV head is shared across num_heads/kv_num_heads query heads). Since hipDNN's graph.sdpa() natively supports different head counts for Q vs K/V, GQA should work via the existing SDPA support without extra op implementations. This bead only adds tests — no new op support code should be needed. Test configurations should include: standard GQA (e.g. 8 query heads, 2 KV heads), MQA (1 KV head), and standard MHA (equal heads) as a baseline.

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: Implementation complete. Code compiles cleanly on both presets (non-MLIR and MLIR). EP runtime tests cannot run due to pre-existing hipBLAS-LT header build failure (same on main). Code review: 2 rounds, 1 critical + 2 generalization issues found and fixed. Ready for review.

title: Add support for int4 RHS / fp16 LHS MatMul through hipDNN
description: Implement support for mixed-precision MatMul where the left-hand side is fp16 and the right-hand side is int4 (quantized weights). This is the pattern used by MatMulNBits (com.microsoft.MatMulNBits) in ONNX Runtime for weight-only quantization in LLM inference. Investigate how this is represented in ORT (likely MatMulNBits with K, N, bits=4, block_size), implement support through hipDNN's graph API or hipBLAS-LT as appropriate, and add tests covering the basic case. This is critical for efficient LLM inference with quantized models.

title: Add support for RoPE (Rotary Positional Embedding) via hipDNN custom op
description: Implement RoPE (Rotary Positional Embedding) support in the EP using a custom op in hipDNN. RoPE applies rotary position encodings to Q and K tensors in attention layers. Investigate how RoPE is represented in ONNX Runtime (likely com.microsoft.RotaryEmbedding), and implement support by mapping it to a hipDNN custom op. This is commonly used in transformer models (LLaMA, etc.) and is a key component for attention-based inference.

title: Add support for Simplified Layer normalization in the EP
description: It is not clear what exactly qualifies as Simplified Layer Normalization, but find out what computation this is in ONNX Runtime and support it through the EP

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text:
Research complete: SimplifiedLayerNormalization is RMS Norm (Y = X * Scale / sqrt(mean(X^2) + epsilon)) — no mean subtraction, no bias. It is a com.microsoft contrib op in ORT, registered in kOnnxDomain. Inputs: X, Scale. Outputs: Y, optional inv_std_var. Attributes: axis, epsilon, stash_type.

hipDNN frontend HAS native RMSNormNode (Graph::rmsnorm(x, scale, attrs)) with RMSNormAttributes supporting inference and training phases.

However, the EP's backend engine (FUSILLI_ENGINE) does NOT support RMSNorm — fusilli only has LayerNormNode (standard layer norm with mean subtraction). Decomposition into supported fusilli ops also fails because RSQRT/SQRT are commented out in fusilli's pointwise modes.

Blocked until fusilli adds one of: (1) native RMSNorm node support, or (2) RSQRT/SQRT pointwise ops (enabling decomposition).

title: Add support for SDPA (Scaled Dot Product Attention) operation in the EP
description: Implement support for the MultiHeadAttention/SDPA contrib op using hipDNN's graph.sdpa() API. The hipDNN SDPA API takes Q, K, V tensors with shape [B, H, S, D] and supports causal masking, variable sequence lengths, attention bias, and different num_heads for Q vs KV (enabling GQA/MQA). For inference, use SdpaAttributes with appropriate settings. The EP should recognize com.microsoft.MultiHeadAttention and map it to graph.sdpa(). Implementation MUST use the hipDNN API (graph.sdpa()), not custom HIP kernels.

Code review: 2 critical + 2 important issues found and fixed (shared attr mutation, fragile attention_bias index, missing Fusilli comment, missing cross-attention test). Ready for review.

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: PR: https://github.com/MaheshRavishankar/hipDNNEP/pull/36

title: Split torch_mlir_graph library to decouple MLIR passes from hipDNN runtime
description:
hipdnn-ep-opt crashes at startup because it transitively links the hipDNN runtime (via hipdnn_ep::src::hipdnn_graph), which pulls in libamd_comgr.so → Rock's libLLVM.so. This conflicts with torch-mlir's statically-linked LLVM (different ABI).

The fix: split src/torch_mlir_graph into two libraries:
1. **torch_mlir_passes** — contains offload, backend_legalize, finalize_memrefs passes + passes.cc (registration). Links hipdnn_dialect and MLIR/torch-mlir libs. Does NOT link hipdnn_graph or hipDNN runtime.
2. **torch_mlir_graph** — contains hipdnn_graph_to_executable_pass.cc and ir_builder.cc. Links hipdnn_ep::src::hipdnn_graph (hipDNN runtime).

passes.cc needs refactoring: buildOffloadPipeline() creates the graph-to-executable pass (needs hipDNN handle) and registerPasses() calls hipdnnCreate(). These runtime-dependent parts must move to torch_mlir_graph or be behind a registration interface.

hipdnn-ep-opt links only torch_mlir_passes (no hipDNN runtime → no comgr → no LLVM conflict). The EP .so links both.

Key files:
- src/torch_mlir_graph/CMakeLists.txt — split library targets
- src/torch_mlir_graph/passes.cc — decouple buildOffloadPipeline/registerPasses from hipDNN runtime
- tools/hipdnn_ep_opt/CMakeLists.txt — link only torch_mlir_passes
- tools/hipdnn_ep_opt/hipdnn-ep-opt.cc — register only non-runtime passes

comments.text: PR: https://github.com/MaheshRavishankar/hipDNNEP/pull/39

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: PR #39 approach rejected: splitting the library and disabling tests is wrong. The real fix: load libhipdnn_backend.so via dlopen(RTLD_LOCAL) instead of direct linking. This keeps comgr's libLLVM.so symbols private and prevents ABI conflicts with torch-mlir's LLVM. No library split needed, no tests disabled. Need to rework hipdnn_graph to dlopen the hipDNN backend.

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: Implementation complete (reworked approach). dlopen(RTLD_LOCAL) shim for libhipdnn_backend.so. All 6 lit tests pass (offload, backend_legalize, finalize_memrefs, bufferize, graph_to_executable, offload_pipeline). Non-MLIR compile succeeds (runtime tests blocked by pre-existing blas_graph SDK issue). Code review: 3 important issues found and fixed. Ready for review.

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: Research complete: hipDNN frontend Graph API does not expose a reduction() method. Only Fusilli's internal Graph class has graph.reduction(). The EP links against hipdnn_frontend (dist), not Fusilli directly. Blocked until hipDNN frontend exposes the reduction API from Fusilli.

title: Allow hipDNN to select engine per-op instead of forcing Fusilli for all operations
description: Currently the EP forces FUSILLI_ENGINE for the entire graph (hipdnn_graph.cc line 1123: graph_->set_preferred_engine_id_ext("FUSILLI_ENGINE")). This blocks operations that hipDNN frontend supports but Fusilli doesn't (e.g. reduction, RMS norm). Change the engine selection so that Fusilli is preferred for conv/gemm/pointwise but other ops let hipDNN pick the best available engine. This may require per-node engine hints or removing the global engine override and letting the backend choose.

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: Clarification: Fusilli should still be preferred for conv/gemm/pointwise graphs. Only skip it when the graph contains ops that Fusilli doesn't support (e.g. reduction, RMS norm). The fix should conditionally set the engine, not remove it entirely.

title: Generalize test base class to reduce boilerplate across all EP tests
description: PR #37 (GQA tests) introduced sdpa_test_base.h as a shared test fixture for MHA and GQA tests. Generalize this into a common EP test base class that can be used by ALL test files (conv, pointwise, matmul, layer norm, MHA/GQA, etc.) to reduce boilerplate. The base class should handle common setup: loading ONNX models, creating ORT sessions with the hipDNN EP registered, running inference, and comparing results against the CPU EP. Study the existing test files to identify the repeated patterns and extract them into a shared fixture.
